rules.rule_text: Consolidate basement car parking areas under building footprints to maximise the area available for deep soil planting beneath forecourts and courtyards.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Maximise the efficiency of car park design with predominantly orthogonal geometry and related to circulation and car space sizes.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Design parking structures which minimise reliance on artificial lighting and car exhaust ventilation.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Provide 1-2% readily accessible parking spaces, designed and appropriately signed for use by people with disabilities.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Provide separate parking for motorcycles for an area equal to 1 car parking space, as a minimum, for every 50 car parking spaces provided, or part thereof. Motor cycle parking does not contribute to the number of parking spaces for the purpose of complying with the maximum number of parking spaces permitted.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: On-site parking must meet the relevant Australian Standard (AS 2890.1 2004 – Parking facilities, or as amended).

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Provide marked pedestrian pathways to car parking areas with clear lines of sight and safe lighting especially at night.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Make provision for secure bicycle parking in all public car parks and every building with onsite parking, in compliance with Part 6 – Traffic and Transport of this DCP.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Bicycle parking in public car parks will achieve safe, easy, and convenient access from the building to public streets.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: For commercial and retail development providing employment for 20 persons or more, provide adequate change and shower facilities for cyclists. Facilities should be conveniently located close to bike storage areas.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_title: Parking for commercial developments and mixed use developments
rules.rule_text: The impact of any at-grade car parking must be minimised by:

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_title: Parking for commercial developments and mixed use developments
rules.rule_text: Natural ventilation should be provided to underground parking areas where possible, with ventilation grilles and structures;

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: To provide car parking in an efficient and cost-effective manner.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Ensure the manner in which the car parking is provided maintains and improves the amenity, aesthetic quality and liveability of the public domain.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Provide car parking in a manner that would make a reduction in the amount and rate of car parking provision possible as the city economy strengthens and alternative modes of transport are developed to serve the city.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Design car parking to be energy efficient, well lit, safe, and attractive.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: The preferred location of car parking in the Auto Alley (West) is in basements. Above ground car parking may be appropriate for some sites, especially where there are constraints such as flood levels and/or archaeological conditions. Above ground car parking will only be permitted where the car parking:

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Car parking areas:

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: To facilitate adaptation of car parking to other uses in the long term, consideration will be given to car parking remaining as part of the common property and not part of, or attached to, individual strata units.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_group: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_text: To facilitate the efficient use of under-occupied car parking spaces within existing commercial buildings in the Auto Alley (West).

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_group: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_text: To appropriately regulate and manage the use of Auto Alley (West) parking spaces in a manner that responds to the changing demand for car parking over time.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_group: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_text: To encourage greater use of under-utilised car parking so as to increase the availability of short term parking in other locations in the Auto Alley (West).

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_group: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_text: The number of surplus spaces in the building must be specified, justified and shown on a site plan submitted with the Development Application. The number of surplus spaces represents the number of spaces above the maximum number required for the floorspace in the building based on the current car parking rates.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_group: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_text: There is demand for take up of this car parking by other commercial enterprises within the Auto Alley (West).

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_group: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_text: The car parking layout and circulation routes, both pedestrian and vehicular are safe and suitable.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_group: 9B.4.6 LEASING OF EXISTING SURPLUS COMMERCIAL CAR PARKING SPACES
rules.rule_text: To promote the orderly and efficient use of surplus parking, spaces will only be permitted to be leased for long term parking (a minimum continuous period of one month).

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: To ensure landscaping is integrated into the design of development within the Auto Alley (West).

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: To encourage well designed landscaping that ameliorates heat bank effects in the Auto Alley (West).

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Commercial and retail developments are to incorporate planting in accessible outdoor spaces such as courtyards, forecourts, terraces and roofs.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: A landscape concept plan must be provided for all landscaped areas. The plan must outline how landscaped areas are to be maintained for the life of the development.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Street trees are to be provided in the footpath in accordance with the street tree mapping in Council’s Parramatta Public Domain Guidelines.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Landscaping of city buildings should consider the use of ‘green walls’ in appropriate locations.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Basement car parks should be contained predominantly within building footprints to allow for deep soil beneath forecourts and courtyards for canopy tree planting.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Design for optimum conditions for plant growth by:

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Design planters to support the appropriate soil depth and plant selection by:

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Provide sufficient soil depth and area to allow for plant establishment and growth. The following minimum standards are recommended: Table 9B.5.2.1 – Minimum soil depth for plant establishment

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Buildings are encouraged to include a green roof component on the roof space.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: To increase the resilience of the City to interruptions in supply and during droughts by providing an alternative water supply to City buildings in the Auto Alley (West).

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: To defer the need to invest in new potable water supply infrastructure to supply future demand in the City.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: To support the recycled water targets of the State Government’s ‘Metropolitan Water Plan’.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Dual reticulation (dual pipe) systems should be installed in new commercial, industrial and mixed use buildings, with the dual reticulation system being of sufficient size to supply all nonpotable water uses of the building.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Use of building or precinct level water harvesting/treatment systems to reduce or eliminate non-potable water demand is encouraged.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Identify appropriate locations and trading hours for late night trading premises in the Parramatta City Centre.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Encourage suitable intensity and growth of late night trading premises in appropriate locations.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Ensure that impacts from late night trading premises are managed to protect a reasonable level of amenity for surrounding residential or sensitive land uses, that is commensurate with its context.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Identify approaches, responsibilities and standards to managing noise and sound.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Ensure that operators of late night trading premises commit to good management practices, to promote a safe night time economy.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Provide the opportunity for premises to extend trading hours where they have demonstrated ongoing good management practices during trial periods.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Encourage late night trading premises that contribute to vibrancy throughout different times of the night (early evening, twilight, night time and late night), as appropriate to the status of the centres within which it is located.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Encourage a broad and inclusive mix of night time uses that reflect the diverse needs of people who work, live and visit City of Parramatta, including dining, drinking, retail, performance, creative and cultural uses.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Trading hours are to be consistent with the base and extended hours that apply to each Late Night Trading Area as outlined in Table 10.3.1.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Trading hours beyond the base hours in Table 10.3.1 are subject to merit assessment and trial periods as set out in Section 10.3.2 – Extended Trading Hours and Trial Periods. Note: Where the base hours are set as 24 hours, no merit assessment or trial period is required.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.3.2 EXTENDED TRADING HOURS AND TRIAL PERIODS
rules.rule_group: 10.3.2 EXTENDED TRADING HOURS AND TRIAL PERIODS
rules.rule_text: Any extended hours beyond base hours will be subject to the following trial periods: a) an initial 2 year trial period; and b) a further 5 year trial period (if the first trial period is successful as determined by Council). Trial periods may be cancelled in the event of poor venue management during the trial period.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.3.2 EXTENDED TRADING HOURS AND TRIAL PERIODS
rules.rule_group: 10.3.2 EXTENDED TRADING HOURS AND TRIAL PERIODS
rules.rule_text: At the completion of each trial period an application to modify the development consent must be lodged to amend the trial period condition. The assessment of the modification application will consider:

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.3.2 EXTENDED TRADING HOURS AND TRIAL PERIODS
rules.rule_group: 10.3.2 EXTENDED TRADING HOURS AND TRIAL PERIODS
rules.rule_text: Venue operators are required to demonstrate compliance with the approved Plan of Management and any other conditions of consent or relevant legislation. Operators must maintain a level of amenity and safety in the vicinity of the premises which is at an acceptable community standard.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.3.2 EXTENDED TRADING HOURS AND TRIAL PERIODS
rules.rule_group: 10.3.2 EXTENDED TRADING HOURS AND TRIAL PERIODS
rules.rule_text: Where a second trial period or permanent extended hours apply, venue operators must review their Plan of Management and make necessary revisions to address concerns raised by Council, NSW Police, Liquor and Gaming NSW, and the community prior to approval by Council.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Noise arising from Entertainment Noise as defined in the Glossary of Terms as noise emanating from activities associated with the Night Time Economy, including music and/or patron noise, will be regulated as follows:

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.2 NOISE CATEGORIES AND LEVELS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.2 NOISE CATEGORIES AND LEVELS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: Entertainment Noise should not exceed the External Noise Category Cumulative Levels in Table 10.4.2 for the respective Noise Categories as shown in Figure 10.4.2.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.2 NOISE CATEGORIES AND LEVELS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.2 NOISE CATEGORIES AND LEVELS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: External Noise Category Cumulative Levels apply: a) on all days. b) to the frontage or boundary of lots. c) to all floors to the maximum LEP building heights.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.2 NOISE CATEGORIES AND LEVELS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.2 NOISE CATEGORIES AND LEVELS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: A 5dB reduction from external cumulative noise levels will be applied to determine the permitted noise contribution for an individual noise generating development.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: A Noise Impact Assessment prepared by suitably qualified acoustic consultant may be required when submitting a development application for new Receiver development where Noise Category A to C (NC) is defined in Figure 10.4.2 – Noise Category Levels in the City Centre and immediate surrounds.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: The Noise Impact Assessment is to:

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: For building facades not facing a defined NC road, such as rear or side facades of a building, a 5dB reduction in Noise Category Cumulative Noise Level shall be applied to account for shielding and reduced exposure to Entertainment Noise.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: Where the New Receiver development is to adjoin an existing venue, the Noise Impact Assessment must quantify emissions from the venue for the purpose of assessment and include consideration of vibration and structure-borne noise.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: Where noise attenuation measures impact the provision of passive natural ventilation, alternative ventilation complying with the National Construction Code must be provided.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: Noise impact from other sources, such as road and rail must be assessed separately in accordance with relevant Standards and policies.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: Noise emission from building services or other sources typically assessed in accordance with the NSW Noise Policy for Industry (NPfI) must comply with the amenity requirements only. No correction to amenity levels is to be applied for high traffic noise environments.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.3 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW RECEIVER DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: For mixed-used development, where some levels may include future venues, the Noise Impact Assessment must identify noise mitigation provisions to enable compliance with the Acoustic requirements for emitters set out in Table 10.4.3 – Mandatory Receiver Internal Entertainment Noise Criteria Leq(15minute)

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.4 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW EMITTERS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.4 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW EMITTERS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: A Noise Impact Assessment prepared by suitably qualified acoustic consultant is required when submitting a development application for new emitter uses.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.4 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW EMITTERS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.4 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW EMITTERS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: The Plan of Management will be used both in the development assessment process and as a means to identify the way in which the premises will operate in compliance to conditions of consent. The Plan of Management will be incorporated as a condition of development consent.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.4 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW EMITTERS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.4 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW EMITTERS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: The Plan of Management should detail all noise management measures to ensure that the development can achieve the Venue Noise Criteria.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.4 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW EMITTERS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.4 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW EMITTERS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: Entertainment Noise from all emitters must not exceed the Venue Noise Criteria, equivalent to the Noise Category Cumulative Levels specified in Table 10.4.2 minus 5dB by reference to Figure 10.4.2 – Noise Category Levels in the City Centre and immediate surrounds , at any surrounding premise lot boundary, 1.5 metres above the floor level of all floors up to the maximum allowable building height, as defined in the LEP.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.section: 10.4.4 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW EMITTERS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_group: 10.4.4 ACOUSTIC REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW EMITTERS WITHIN LNTAS
rules.rule_text: Entertainment Noise from emitters with an adjoining Receiver, that may be affected by noise transfer via the common partition must not exceed the Receiver Internal Noise Criteria specified in 4.3 minus 5dB as well as Table 10.4.4 minus 5dB at the most-potentially affected location (no less than 1 metre) from the common partition.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Ensure that the potential impacts from the operation of the premises are considered and addressed during the assessment of an application;

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Enable Council to review Plans of Management to ensure that management practices are being appropriately applied to late night trading premises.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: Provide certainty for both the consent authority, operators and the local community about the ongoing management practices to be employed by the proposed use to manage its impact upon the neighbourhood.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: A Plan of Management must be completed in accordance with the guidelines in Section 10.5.1 and is required to accompany an application for the following applications:

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: An assessment of the social impacts of the proposal should be included:

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: The Plan of Management will be used both in the development assessment process and as a means to identify the way in which the premises will operate in compliance to conditions of consent. The Plan of Management will be incorporated as a condition of development consent.

rules.development_group: Parramatta City Centre – Auto Alley West
rules.rule_text: The Plan of Management should detail all noise management measures to ensure that the development can achieve the Venue Noise Criteria.
